overview: The Windows Registry functions similarly to a file system. Understanding its structure helps in identifying potential persistence mechanisms.

workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.

tips: Since registry modification auditing is not enabled by default, administrators must configure System Access Control Lists (SACLs) to monitor specific registry keys.
